Do not quote to buyers. $42k rep quarterly goal Tool 01 Revenue Math sidebar Rep quota (manager-owned) Internal assumption — Internal only. Informed Sport Certified Card #4 Regulatory Proof; Objection Matrix Row 4 Founder transcript (¶95): “Restless is Informed Sport Certified. They're third-party verified, recognized by the DOD” Verified — founder 2026-05-18 OK to cite. Confirm cert is current for the SKU before written quotes. DOD banned-substance status Card #4 Regulatory Proof; Objection Matrix Row 4 Founder transcript (¶91): “I have no ingredients on that list” (DOD banned-substance list) Verified — clear, not certified 2026-05-18 OK to say “clear of DOD banned substances.” Do not say “OPSS-approved” or “OPSS-certified” — OPSS is the program name, not a certification mark. NSF certification Card #4 Regulatory Proof; Objection Matrix Row 4 Not stated in available sources Needs confirmation — Do not claim NSF unless cert doc is in hand. Named-account case studies (fire station, MWR, etc.) Story Vault; Card #5 Trial Mechanics No attributable, written case study on file at v4.2 release Needs confirmation — Do not cite as “we did X at Y.” Use Card #5 Trial Mechanics framing. Customer testimonials (~130 five-star reviews / ~450 orders) Card #5 Trial Mechanics; Tool 09 F-band Founder transcript (¶77): “130 five-star reviews out of about 450 orders” (on the website) Verified — founder 2026-05-18 OK to say “~130 five-star reviews on the website.” Do not extrapolate to clinical or shift-performance claims. Functional ingredients (Rhodiola rosea, prebiotic fiber, magnesium bisglycinate, ~27 ingredients) Tool 09 F1; Card #2; Card #6 Science Brief Founder transcript (¶117): “Rhodiola rosea”, “prebiotic fiber”, “magnesium bisglycinate”, “27 quality ingredients” Verified — founder 2026-05-18 OK to name ingredients. Do not assert clinical outcomes. Functional benefits stay at ingredient-level research, not product claims. Founder backstory (Marine veteran, MCRD, dad in construction, brother is police officer) Card #1 Founder Story; Story Vault Card E Founder transcript (¶11, ¶25, ¶45, ¶55) Verified — founder 2026-05-18 OK to use. Story is backdrop, not the pitch — return to the buyer's shift, crew, or trial. SOCOM contract evaluation Internal coaching only — not in this manual Founder transcript (¶99): “I don't know if I can say that” Internal only — do not cite 2026-05-18 Do not cite externally in any form. Founder explicitly hedged on disclosure. "Stay in the Fight" brand line Cover; Tool 03 brand moments Internal marketing (Restless) Verified — internal — OK in responder / military / industrial. Do not lead healthcare buyers with it — see Card #4 and APX H. Rule: Any claim marked Needs confirmation stays out of email, deck, and written follow-up until status changes. Verbal use must include the hedge phrase. The rep's job is to protect the buyer from claims the label can't support — and to protect Restless from claims it hasn't earned. manualPages.label: Appendix H · Healthcare Evidence Pack Stub
manualPages.title: Healthcare · Label First, Evidence First .
manualPages.text: Appendix H · Healthcare Evidence Pack Stub Healthcare · Label First, Evidence First . Appendix H 29 / 30 Healthcare Path This page is a stub, not the evidence pack itself. It defines the rep's healthcare path so we do not pretend a fully-embedded pack lives in this manual. The actual evidence pack is owned and updated by the manager. Healthcare Operating Rules 1. Healthcare buyers receive evidence and label materials before any trial. No sticks ship first. 2. Do not lead with "Stay in the Fight." Lead with label, evidence, and review process. 3. Do not claim certification, approval, or clinical outcome unless the certifying document is verified and current. 4. Use Card #6 Science Brief for internal coaching only. Do not quote it to healthcare buyers. 5. Procurement / nursing admin / charge nurse drive the gate — not the rep. If they ask for a trial, that's the signal. Until then, evidence-only. If you are about to ship trial sticks to a healthcare account without an evidence-and-label review on file: stop. Code the deal as "Paused – awaiting review" and route through the manager. That is the discipline. Evidence Pack — Where It Lives Asset Owner / Location Status Supplement Facts panel (current SKU) Manager-provided Send before any trial discussion. Ingredient transparency one-pager Manager-provided Pair with the panel. Current certification status (NSF / Informed Sport / OPSS) Manager-provided — status varies by SKU Send only if verified current. Reviewed claim guardrails (Card #4 + Objection Matrix Row 4) This manual (· Appendix E, F) Carry in field. COA (Certificate of Analysis) Manager-provided on request Send only on procurement request. If your manager has not handed you the current evidence pack, you do not have a healthcare account — you have a lead. Ask before the meeting. Healthcare Talk Track — Default "Restless is one stick: caffeine, L-theanine, electrolytes, on one label. Before any conversation about a trial, I'd like to walk you through the Supplement Facts panel and the review materials. Once you've had a chance to review, you tell me if and when a trial fits your standard." Appendix H — Healthcare Evidence Pack Stub 29 / 30

objections.safeRoute: Has a coach or alpha tried it visibly?
objections.bridge: We protect the champion's credibility. Start with the alpha.

objections.decode: Stall · not interested · needs social proof
objections.safeRoute: What info would change a trial to yes?
objections.bridge: I'll bring the label, a sample, and a case study. 10 minutes.

objections.safeRoute: What does approval mean for your process?
objections.bridge: I won't claim certification I can't verify. Here's the label and current status.

objections.safeRoute: Cost per serving vs. what the crew buys now?
objections.bridge: One stick replaces the coffee + hydration + pre-workout stack.

objections.safeRoute: What does the crew need at hour 6 — a spike or a window?
objections.bridge: Designed for a steadier use-window test, not a spike.

objections.safeRoute: What does the crew do at hour 3?
objections.bridge: Different format: one stick in water, with the label visible.

objections.safeRoute: Founder story + evidence pack + retime if needed
objections.bridge: Fair. We were built for this market, not the grocery aisle.

activationStandard.label: Named human
activationStandard.requiredAnswer: First name, last name, role, direct contact.

activationStandard.label: Current routine
activationStandard.requiredAnswer: What they use now in their words.

activationStandard.label: Real use window
activationStandard.requiredAnswer: Exact shift, training, duty day, hot shift, evidence review, or event.

activationStandard.label: Buyer-named risk
activationStandard.requiredAnswer: Taste, policy, crew rejection, dead inventory, budget, certification, credibility, format, or other.

activationStandard.label: Trial / review type
activationStandard.requiredAnswer: Station Bag, Coach Trial, Single-Stick Trial, Break Room Box, Evidence Pack, or Event Pack.

activationStandard.label: Binary success question + route
activationStandard.requiredAnswer: Would you use it again for the same window? plus route on yes and route on no.

exitReasons.trigger: Let me know how it goes
exitReasons.reengageWhen: They commit to a check-in date.
Counter: 'Start with the alpha. Visible win first.'
